name: Icinga2 REST API
what_it_does: Open-source infrastructure monitoring platform with a native REST API for managing hosts, services, downtimes, acknowledgements, and querying real-time check results.
use_cases: Automating downtime scheduling for maintenance windows from deployment agents
Querying current service status and check results for NOC automation
Acknowledging alerts programmatically when incidents are detected in other systems
Adding and removing monitored hosts dynamically as infrastructure scales
Triggering on-demand checks and reading results for real-time health validation
not_for: Organizations starting from scratch with modern stacks
Application-level tracing and APM (Icinga focuses on infrastructure)
best_when: An agent automates operations for organizations with existing Icinga2 monitoring infrastructure running on-premises or in hybrid environments.
avoid_when: You are building a new observability stack without legacy Nagios/Icinga infrastructure to maintain.

auth.notes: API users with configurable permissions defined in icinga2 configuration. Basic auth over HTTPS. API users limited to specific object types and actions via permissions config.

pricing.paid_tiers: Icinga for Windows and enterprise support: custom pricing
pricing.notes: Core Icinga2 is entirely free and open-source. Commercial modules (Director, Icinga DB) have open-source versions. Enterprise support contracts available.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documentation is thorough with examples for all operations. Error responses are JSON with descriptive messages. Filter syntax (Icinga filter expressions) requires learning but is powerful.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Object creation is idempotent if using consistent names. Downtime scheduling and acknowledgements can be checked before creating duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: Filter syntax uses Icinga2 language expressions, not standard query formats
API must be explicitly enabled in icinga2.conf — not enabled by default
TLS certificate validation required by default — self-signed certs require configuration
Object changes via API don't persist across restarts without a config director

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced for API communication. API user permissions configurable per object type. Open-source codebase is auditable. Basic auth model is less modern than token-based approaches.
